SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.execution

Class SessionContext

java.lang.Object
org.apache.arrow.datafusion.execution.SessionContext
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable
public class SessionContext extends Object implements AutoCloseable
A session context for executing DataFusion queries using the FFM API.

This class wraps a native DataFusion SessionContext and provides methods for registering tables and executing SQL queries.

Example: 

import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

try (SessionContext ctx = new SessionContext()) {
    ctx.registerCsv("sales", "/data/sales.csv", CsvReadOptions.defaults(), allocator);
    DataFrame result = ctx.sql("SELECT region, SUM(amount) FROM sales GROUP BY region")
        .sort(col("region").sortAsc())
        .limit(0, 10);
    result.show();
}
See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext

    Creates a new session context with default configuration.
    SessionContext(ConfigOptions config)

  • Constructor Details

    • SessionContext

      public SessionContext()
      Creates a new session context with default configuration.

    • SessionContext

      public SessionContext(ConfigOptions config)
      Creates a new session context with the specified configuration.
      Parameters:
      config - the session configuration

  • Method Details

    • create

      public static SessionContext create()
      Creates a new session context with default configuration.

      Equivalent to new SessionContext(). 

      SessionContext ctx = SessionContext.create();
      Returns:
      a new SessionContext
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::new

    • newWithConfig

      public static SessionContext newWithConfig(ConfigOptions config)
      Creates a new session context with the specified configuration.

      Equivalent to Rust's SessionContext::new_with_config(config). 

      ConfigOptions config = ConfigOptions.defaults();
SessionContext ctx = SessionContext.newWithConfig(config);
      Parameters:
      config - the session configuration
      Returns:
      a new SessionContext
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::new_with_config

    • newWithConfigRt

      public static SessionContext newWithConfigRt(ConfigOptions config, RuntimeEnv runtimeEnv)
      Creates a new session context with the specified configuration and runtime environment.

      This mirrors Rust's SessionContext::new_with_config_rt(config, Arc<RuntimeEnv>). The RuntimeEnv is cloned internally, so the caller retains ownership and must close it separately.

      Example: 

      RuntimeEnv rt = RuntimeEnvBuilder.builder()
    .withMemoryLimit(50_000_000, 1.0)
    .build();
ConfigOptions config = ConfigOptions.defaults();
try (SessionContext ctx = SessionContext.newWithConfigRt(config, rt)) {
    DataFrame df = ctx.sql("SELECT 1 + 1");
}
rt.close();
      Parameters:
      config - the session configuration
      runtimeEnv - the runtime environment (e.g., with memory pool configuration)
      Returns:
      a new SessionContext
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if context creation fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::new_with_config_rt

    • newWithFederation

      public static SessionContext newWithFederation(ConfigOptions config)
      Creates a new session context with federation support enabled.

      Federation-aware providers (for example, org.apache.arrow.datafusion.providers.flight.FlightSqlFederatedCatalog) push filters, projections, joins, and aggregates down to their remote systems. Providers registered with this context that are not federation-aware behave exactly as they do in a regular session — the federation optimizer and planner only rewrite plans whose scans resolve to a FederationProvider. 

      try (SessionContext ctx = SessionContext.newWithFederation(ConfigOptions.defaults())) {
    // Register federated catalogs / schemas here ...
    DataFrame df = ctx.sql("SELECT * FROM remote1.public.t1 JOIN remote2.public.t2 USING (id)");
    df.show();
}
      Parameters:
      config - the session configuration
      Returns:
      a new SessionContext wired with FederationOptimizerRule and FederatedQueryPlanner
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if context creation fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::new_with_state

    • registerBatch

      public void registerBatch(String name, org.apache.arrow.vector.VectorSchemaRoot root, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Registers a VectorSchemaRoot as a table in the session context.

      This is equivalent to Rust's register_batch: it takes Arrow data directly and wraps it in a MemTable. 

      try (SessionContext ctx = new SessionContext()) {
    ctx.registerBatch("my_table", vectorSchemaRoot, allocator);
    DataFrame df = ctx.sql("SELECT * FROM my_table");
    df.show();
}
      Parameters:
      name - The table name
      root - The VectorSchemaRoot containing the data
      allocator - The buffer allocator
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if registration fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::register_batch

    • registerBatch

      public void registerBatch(String name, org.apache.arrow.vector.VectorSchemaRoot root, org.apache.arrow.vector.dictionary.DictionaryProvider provider, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Registers a VectorSchemaRoot as a table in the session context with dictionary support.

      This is equivalent to Rust's register_batch: it takes Arrow data directly and wraps it in a MemTable. See registerBatch(String, VectorSchemaRoot, BufferAllocator) for an example.

      Parameters:
      name - The table name
      root - The VectorSchemaRoot containing the data
      provider - The DictionaryProvider for dictionary-encoded columns (may be null)
      allocator - The buffer allocator
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if registration fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::register_batch

    • registerTable

      public void registerTable(String name, TableProvider provider, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Registers a table provider with the session context.

      This is equivalent to Rust's register_table: it registers a TableProvider implementation that DataFusion will call when the table is queried. 

      try (SessionContext ctx = new SessionContext()) {
    ctx.registerTable("my_table", myTableProvider, allocator);
    DataFrame df = ctx.sql("SELECT * FROM my_table");
    df.show();
}
      Parameters:
      name - The table name
      provider - The table provider implementation
      allocator - The buffer allocator to use for Arrow data transfers
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if registration fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::register_table

    • registerTable

      public void registerTable(String name, RustTableProvider provider)
      Registers a Rust-side table provider (for example one produced by FlightSqlTableProvider.builder()).

      The provider handle remains owned by the caller — registering it does not transfer ownership, and callers must still close the handle when done. 

      try (FlightSqlTableProvider taxi = FlightSqlTableProvider.builder()
        .endpoint("http://localhost:32010")
        .query("SELECT * FROM taxi")
        .build()) {
    ctx.registerTable("taxi", taxi);
    ctx.sql("SELECT count(*) FROM taxi").show();
}
      Parameters:
      name - the table name used in SQL
      provider - a Rust-backed table provider
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if registration fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::register_table

    • deregisterTable

      public boolean deregisterTable(String name)
      Deregisters a table by name. 
      boolean existed = ctx.deregisterTable("my_table");
      Parameters:
      name - The table name
      Returns:
      true if a table was previously registered with this name
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if deregistration fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::deregister_table

    • sql

      public DataFrame sql(String query)
      Executes a SQL query and returns a DataFrame. 
      DataFrame df = ctx.sql("SELECT name, age FROM users WHERE age > 21");
df.show();
      Parameters:
      query - The SQL query to execute
      Returns:
      A DataFrame representing the query result
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if query execution fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::sql

    • executeLogicalPlan

      public DataFrame executeLogicalPlan(LogicalPlan plan)
      Executes a LogicalPlan and returns a DataFrame.

      The plan is cloned internally, so the same plan can be executed multiple times. 

      LogicalPlan plan = LogicalPlanBuilder.scan("my_table", ctx)
    .filter(col("id").gt(lit(100)))
    .build();
DataFrame df = ctx.executeLogicalPlan(plan);
df.show();
      Parameters:
      plan - the logical plan to execute
      Returns:
      a DataFrame representing the execution result
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if execution fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::execute_logical_plan

    • registerCatalog

      public void registerCatalog(String name, CatalogProvider catalog, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Registers a catalog provider with the session context.

      The catalog can be accessed in SQL queries using the catalog name. For example, if you register a catalog named "my_catalog" with a schema "my_schema" containing a table "my_table", you can query it with: 

      ctx.registerCatalog("my_catalog", myCatalog, allocator);
DataFrame df = ctx.sql("SELECT * FROM my_catalog.my_schema.my_table");
df.show();
      Parameters:
      name - The catalog name
      catalog - The catalog provider implementation
      allocator - The buffer allocator to use for Arrow data transfers
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if registration fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::register_catalog

    • registerCatalog

      public void registerCatalog(String name, RustCatalogProvider catalog)
      Registers a Rust-side catalog provider (for example one produced by FlightSqlFederatedCatalog.builder()).

      The catalog handle remains owned by the caller — registering it does not transfer ownership, and callers must still close the handle when done. 

      try (FlightSqlFederatedCatalog remote = FlightSqlFederatedCatalog.builder()
        .endpoint("http://localhost:32010")
        .computeContext("remote1")
        .table("users")
        .build()) {
    ctx.registerCatalog("remote1", remote);
    ctx.sql("SELECT * FROM remote1.public.users").show();
}
      Parameters:
      name - the catalog name used in SQL
      catalog - a Rust-backed catalog provider
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if registration fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::register_catalog

    • registerVariable

      public void registerVariable(VarType type, VarProvider provider, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Registers a variable provider for resolving variable references in SQL queries.

      User-defined variables use the @ prefix and system variables use @@. Once registered, queries can reference these variables directly: 

      VarProvider provider = new VarProvider() {
    @Override
    public ScalarValue getValue(List<String> varNames) {
        return new ScalarValue.Utf8("Alice");
    }
    @Override
    public Optional<ArrowType> getType(List<String> varNames) {
        return Optional.of(ArrowType.Utf8.INSTANCE);
    }
};
ctx.registerVariable(VarType.USER_DEFINED, provider, allocator);
DataFrame df = ctx.sql("SELECT @name");
      Parameters:
      type - the variable type (system or user-defined)
      provider - the variable provider implementation
      allocator - the buffer allocator to use for Arrow data transfers
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if registration fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::register_variable

    • registerUdf

      public void registerUdf(ScalarUDF udf, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Registers a scalar UDF with the session context.

      Once registered, the UDF can be used in SQL queries by name. For example: 

      ScalarUDF pow = Functions.createUdf("pow", Volatility.IMMUTABLE,
    List.of(new ArrowType.FloatingPoint(FloatingPointPrecision.DOUBLE),
            new ArrowType.FloatingPoint(FloatingPointPrecision.DOUBLE)),
    new ArrowType.FloatingPoint(FloatingPointPrecision.DOUBLE),
    (args, numRows, alloc) -> { return resultVector; });
ctx.registerUdf(pow, allocator);
DataFrame result = ctx.sql("SELECT pow(a, b) FROM t");
      Parameters:
      udf - The scalar UDF implementation
      allocator - The buffer allocator to use for Arrow data transfers
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if registration fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::register_udf

    • registerUdaf

      public void registerUdaf(AggregateUDF udaf, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Registers an aggregate UDF with the session context.

      Once registered, the UDAF can be used in SQL queries by name. For example: 

      AggregateUDF mySum = new AggregateUDF() {
    public String name() { return "my_sum"; }
    public Signature signature() { return new Signature(Volatility.IMMUTABLE); }
    public Field returnField(List<Field> argFields) {
        return Field.nullable("my_sum", new ArrowType.Int(64, true));
    }
    public Accumulator createAccumulator() { return new MySumAccumulator(); }
};
ctx.registerUdaf(mySum, allocator);
DataFrame result = ctx.sql("SELECT my_sum(value) FROM t GROUP BY key");
      Parameters:
      udaf - The aggregate UDF implementation
      allocator - The buffer allocator to use for Arrow data transfers
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if registration fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::register_udaf

    • registerListingTable

      public void registerListingTable(String name, ListingTable table, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Registers a listing table with the session context.

      A listing table scans files in a directory using a user-provided FileFormat. For example: 

      ListingTableUrl url = ListingTableUrl.parse("/path/to/data/");
ListingOptions options = ListingOptions.builder(myFormat).build();
ListingTableConfig config = new ListingTableConfig(url)
    .withListingOptions(options)
    .withSchema(mySchema);
ListingTable table = new ListingTable(config);
ctx.registerListingTable("my_table", table, allocator);
      Parameters:
      name - The table name
      table - The listing table configuration
      allocator - The buffer allocator to use for Arrow data transfers
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if registration fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::register_listing_table

    • registerCsv

      public void registerCsv(String name, String path, CsvReadOptions options, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Registers a CSV file or directory as a named table. 
      ctx.registerCsv("sales", "/data/sales.csv", CsvReadOptions.defaults(), allocator);
DataFrame df = ctx.sql("SELECT * FROM sales");
      Parameters:
      name - The table name
      path - The file or directory path
      options - CSV read options
      allocator - The buffer allocator
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if registration fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::register_csv

    • registerParquet

      public void registerParquet(String name, String path, ParquetReadOptions options, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Registers a Parquet file or directory as a named table. 
      ctx.registerParquet("events", "/data/events.parquet", ParquetReadOptions.defaults(), allocator);
DataFrame df = ctx.sql("SELECT * FROM events");
      Parameters:
      name - The table name
      path - The file or directory path
      options - Parquet read options
      allocator - The buffer allocator
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if registration fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::register_parquet

    • registerJson

      public void registerJson(String name, String path, NdJsonReadOptions options, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Registers a JSON file or directory as a named table. 
      ctx.registerJson("logs", "/data/logs.json", NdJsonReadOptions.defaults(), allocator);
DataFrame df = ctx.sql("SELECT * FROM logs");
      Parameters:
      name - The table name
      path - The file or directory path
      options - JSON read options
      allocator - The buffer allocator
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if registration fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::register_json

    • registerArrow

      public void registerArrow(String name, String path, ArrowReadOptions options, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Registers an Arrow IPC file or directory as a named table. 
      ctx.registerArrow("events", "/data/events.arrow", ArrowReadOptions.builder().build(), allocator);
DataFrame df = ctx.sql("SELECT * FROM events");
      Parameters:
      name - The table name
      path - The file or directory path
      options - Arrow read options
      allocator - The buffer allocator
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if registration fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::register_arrow

    • tableExist

      public boolean tableExist(String name)
      Returns whether a table with the given name exists. 
      boolean exists = ctx.tableExist("my_table");
      Parameters:
      name - The table name
      Returns:
      true if the table exists, false otherwise
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if the existence check fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::table_exist

    • table

      public Optional<DataFrame> table(String name)
      Creates a DataFrame for a registered table, if it exists.

      Equivalent to Rust's ctx.table("name"). 

      Optional<DataFrame> df = ctx.table("my_table");
df.ifPresent(DataFrame::show);
      Parameters:
      name - The table name
      Returns:
      An Optional containing a DataFrame for the specified table, or empty if not found
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if there is an error other than the table not being found
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::table

    • readParquet

      public DataFrame readParquet(String path)
      Reads a Parquet file/directory into a DataFrame. 
      DataFrame df = ctx.readParquet("/data/events.parquet");
df.show();
      Parameters:
      path - The file or directory path
      Returns:
      A DataFrame for the Parquet data
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if reading fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::read_parquet

    • readParquet

      public DataFrame readParquet(String path, ParquetReadOptions options, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Reads a Parquet file/directory into a DataFrame with options. See readParquet(String) for an example.
      Parameters:
      path - The file or directory path
      options - Parquet read options
      allocator - The buffer allocator (needed if schema is specified in options)
      Returns:
      A DataFrame for the Parquet data
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if reading fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::read_parquet

    • readCsv

      public DataFrame readCsv(String path)
      Reads a CSV file/directory into a DataFrame. 
      DataFrame df = ctx.readCsv("/data/sales.csv");
df.show();
      Parameters:
      path - The file or directory path
      Returns:
      A DataFrame for the CSV data
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if reading fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::read_csv

    • readCsv

      public DataFrame readCsv(String path, CsvReadOptions options, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Reads a CSV file/directory into a DataFrame with options. See readCsv(String) for an example.
      Parameters:
      path - The file or directory path
      options - CSV read options
      allocator - The buffer allocator (needed if schema is specified in options)
      Returns:
      A DataFrame for the CSV data
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if reading fails

      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::read_csv

      public DataFrame readJson(String path)
      Reads a JSON file/directory into a DataFrame. 
      DataFrame df = ctx.readJson("/data/logs.json");
df.show();

      A DataFrame for the JSON data
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if reading fails

    • readArrow

      public DataFrame readArrow(String path)
      Reads an Arrow IPC file/directory into a DataFrame. 
      DataFrame df = ctx.readArrow("/data/events.arrow");
df.show();
      Parameters:
      path - The file or directory path
      Returns:
      A DataFrame for the Arrow data
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if reading fails

      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::read_arrow

    • readArrow

      public DataFrame readArrow(String path, ArrowReadOptions options, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Reads an Arrow IPC file/directory into a DataFrame with options. See readArrow(String) for an example.
      Parameters:
      path - The file or directory path
      options - Arrow read options
      allocator - The buffer allocator (needed if schema is specified in options)
      Returns:
      A DataFrame for the Arrow data
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if reading fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::read_arrow

    • catalogNames

      public List<String> catalogNames()
      Returns the names of all registered catalogs. 
      List<String> names = ctx.catalogNames();
      Returns:
      list of catalog names
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::catalog_names

    • catalog

      public Optional<CatalogProvider> catalog(String name)
      Returns a catalog provider for the named catalog, if it exists.

      The returned provider supports introspection (listing schemas and tables) but does not support retrieving native TableProvider instances. Use table(String) to query native tables. 

      Optional<CatalogProvider> catalog = ctx.catalog("datafusion");
      Parameters:
      name - The catalog name
      Returns:
      An Optional containing the catalog provider, or empty if not found
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::catalog

    • state

      public SessionState state()
      Creates a snapshot of this session's state.

      The returned SessionState owns its own Tokio runtime and can outlive this SessionContext. 

      SessionState state = ctx.state();
      Returns:
      a new SessionState
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if the state cannot be created
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::state

    • parseSqlExpr

      public Expr parseSqlExpr(String sql, org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Schema schema)
      Parses a SQL expression string into an Expr.

      The expression is parsed against the given schema, which defines the available column names and types. For example: 

      Schema schema = new Schema(List.of(
    Field.nullable("a", new ArrowType.Int(32, true)),
    Field.nullable("b", new ArrowType.Int(32, true))));
Expr expr = ctx.parseSqlExpr("a + b > 10", schema);
      Parameters:
      sql - the SQL expression string to parse
      schema - the Arrow schema describing the available columns
      Returns:
      the parsed expression
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if the expression cannot be parsed
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::parse_sql_expr

    • sessionId

      public String sessionId()
      Returns the unique identifier for this session. 
      String id = ctx.sessionId();
      Returns:
      the session ID string
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if the call fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::session_id

    • sessionStartTime

      public Instant sessionStartTime()
      Returns the time this session was created. 
      Instant startTime = ctx.sessionStartTime();
      Returns:
      the session start time as an Instant
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if the call fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SessionContext::session_start_time

    • bridge

      public SessionContextBridge bridge()
      Returns the underlying bridge. Internal use only.

    • close

      public void close()
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
